data.title: Lululemon Stock (LULU) Is Trading Higher Today. Analysts Lift Price Targets but Warn the 'Reset Isn't Over'
data.description:
Lululemon Athletica (LULU) is trading higher on Friday after reporting better-than-expected Q3 FY25 results and confirming that CEO Calvin McDonald will step down at the end of the fiscal year. The beat and the leadership change gave the stock a lift, but analysts say the company is still in the middle of a reset, with key issues in the U.S. business yet to improve.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Telsey Raises Price Target but Sees Ongoing Pressure

Telsey Advisory analyst Dana Telsey raised her Lululemon price target to $215 from $200 while keeping a Market Perform rating. She said the Q3 report looked good, but reminded investors that expectations were already lowered after LULU's weak guide in September.

Telsey pointed out that international markets continue to grow, but the Americas posted a seventh straight quarter of flat-to-down comps. Margins also fell due to tariffs and markdowns, and the company expects even more margin pressure in Q4.

She noted that Lululemon has faced a "challenging year," marked by softer demand, execution missteps, and now several leadership changes. While the interim co-CEOs may help steady the business, Telsey believes real progress depends on the next permanent CEO. She also said sales slowed after Thanksgiving, adding to near-term pressure.

Bernstein Says CEO Change Helps but U.S. Challenges Remain

Meanwhile, Bernstein analyst Aneesha Sherman reiterated her Market Perform rating and $190 price target. She said the CEO's exit may support the stock in the short term, but the underlying issues remain clear: U.S. growth is still negative, and the American Express partnership has not provided a meaningful lift.

Sherman said the biggest question for Lululemon is why its newness strategy has not yet worked. She does not expect clarity until Spring 2026, when early product changes show up in results and the new CEO lays out a clear strategy.

Is LULU Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Overall, Wall Street has a Hold consensus rating on Lululemon Athletica stock based on 15 Holds, one Buy, and one Sell recommendation. The average LULU stock price target of $186.31 indicates shares are fully priced at current levels.

These ratings/price targets might change as more analysts react to Q3 results and the CEO departure.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: 'We See a Compelling Opportunity': Costco Stock (COST) Stalls as Q1 Divides Wall Street
data.description:
Shares in Costco Wholesale Corporation (COST) on Friday shrugged off the membership-based retailer's Q1 2026 earnings beat as Wall Street analysts took opposite sides of the stock.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

During the quarter, the bulk-purchase-focused company grew its revenue by 8.3% from a year ago to $67.31 billion, with digital sales jumping by 20.5%. Its earnings per share also climbed by 11.4% year-over-year to $4.50 per share.

Goldman Sachs Shows 'Confidence' in Costco

However, reacting to the performance, while some analysts raised their price targets on COST stock, others trimmed them. Goldman Sachs analyst Kate McShane reaffirmed her Buy rating on the stock and put forward a price target of $1,171, implying over 32% upside from Thursday's regular session closing price of $884.48.

McShane said her equity research team continues "to have confidence" in Costco's value proposition and sustained resonance with shoppers. She highlighted growth in the company's digital and alternative revenue streams, along with new Costco warehouse locations driving sales and new stores boosting the membership base.

'Recent Pullback Creates Opportunity,' Says TD Cowen

In addition, UBS analyst Michael Lasser noted that "we see a compelling opportunity to own the stock," while TD Cowen analyst Oliver Chen added that the "recent pullback [in the stock price] creates opportunity."

According to Lasser, most of the concerns about Costco, including about traffic, comparable sales, and customer membership renewal rates, were "favorably addressed" during the quarter. The analyst added that his team sees Costco remaining consistent with its growth journey and not slowing down.

Mizuho Says Sales Continue to 'Cannibalize'

On the other end, Mizuho analyst David Bellinger stuck to his Neutral rating on the stock. Bellinger called Costco's earnings beat "modest" and noted that the retailer's operating expenditures are still rising — albeit at a slower pace — and that the rate of increase in paying Costco members has also slowed compared with the past 14 quarters.

Furthermore, the Mizuho analyst pointed out that the opening of new warehouses by U.S. retailers continues to "cannibalize sales at an elevated level." This means that a meaningful chunk of sales is simply shifting from older warehouse clubs to the newer ones.

Wrapping up, Bellinger noted that although he expects Costco to keep running its business model "incredibly well," he and his team "await a better entry point."

Is COST Stock a Good Buy?

Across Wall Street, Costco's shares hold a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 16 Buys and eight Holds issued by analysts over the past three months.

At $1,091.79, the average COST price target implies more than a 24% upswing possibility.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Fed's Goolsbee, Schmid Dissented Against a Rate Cut. Here's Why
data.description:
On Wednesday, the Fed voted 9-3 to cut interest rates by 25 bps. Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee and Kansas City Fed President Jeffrey Schmid dissented in favor of holding rates steady, while Fed Governor Stephen Miran voted for a 50 bps reduction.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

The risk of persistent inflation and the lack of data from the government shutdown was a major factor behind Goolsbee's decision. "But I've just been uncomfortable front-loading too many rate cuts and assuming that what we've seen in inflation will be transitory," Goolsbee told CNBC's "Squawk Box" on Friday. He added that he is "optimistic" that rates will be a "fair bit lower" in 2026.

Schmid Argues Economy Still Strong, Inflation Too High

Schmid had a similar reason, saying that elevated inflation, paired with a healthy economy, calls for a modestly restrictive interest rate.

"Inflation remains too high, the economy shows continued momentum, and the labor market—though cooling—remains largely in balance," wrote Schmid in a statement. Furthermore, he believes that the economy hasn't changed much since the Fed voted to cut rates by 25 bps in October.

Core personal consumption expenditures (PCE), the Fed's preferred gauge of inflation, was 2.8% in September, still above the Fed's target of 2%.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: 'Nvidia May Open the Gate': NVDA Weighs Higher H200 Output as China Interest Grows
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) is weighing a plan to boost output of its high-end H200 AI chip after demand from China jumped past current supply, according to a Reuters report. The move comes just days after U.S. President Donald Trump said trump-says-nvidia-can-ship-h200-chips-to-china-other-countries-thefly">Nvidia could export the H200 chip to China, but with a 25% fee on sales. That shift has drawn attention from Chinese firms that want access to advanced AI hardware.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

China Buyers Rush In

Reuters noted that supply of the H200 is still tight, and companies such as Alibaba (BABA) and ByteDance have expressed interest in buying the chip. Notably, Nvidia relies on Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSM) to produce it.

At the same time, China is working to develop its own domestic AI chip sector, and the government has not yet approved any H200 purchases. Reportedly, government agencies held emergency meetings on Wednesday to review the sudden shift in U.S. policy and to weigh the risks and benefits of allowing the chip into the country.

For Nvidia, the stakes are high. The company has long seen strong demand for advanced AI chips in China, but sales were restricted under earlier U.S. export rules. The new policy opens a narrow window for Nvidia to sell one of its most powerful processors into the market, but only if China signs off and only at a steep tariff.

Is NVDA Stock a Buy Now?

Currently, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Nvidia stock based on 39 Buys, one Hold, and one Sell recommendation. The average NVDA stock price target of $258.45 indicates 41.91% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: S&P 500 to Soar to 7,700 Next Year, Says Fundstrat's Tom Lee
data.description:
Fundstrat co-founder and Bitmine Immersion Technologies (BMNR) Chairman Tom Lee expects the bull market's momentum to continue in 2026, with the S&P 500 (SPX) reaching 7,700. That implies upside of 11.6% from Thursday's close.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

"After 3 years >20% yearly gains, bull market still alive," said Lee. "The significant 'Wall of Worry' is a tailwind for bull market." Lee had a SPX price target of 6,600 for 2025, which has already been met.

Lee Recommends Tech and Crypto Stocks

Lee expects the successor of Fed Chair Jerome Powell to have a more dovish monetary policy stance, creating another tailwind for stocks. Powell's term is set to end in May 2026, with many expecting President Trump to nominate National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett to the position.

Furthermore, Lee recommends exposure to tech, crypto, materials, energy, and financial stocks. He joins a growing crowd of Wall Street firms bullish for next year, including Deutsche Bank with a price target of 8,100 and RBC Capital at 7,750.

data.title: RGTI Stock Outlook — Why This Top Analyst Sees Big Upside for Rigetti
data.description:
Rigetti Computing (RGTI) is back on investors' radar after five-star-rated analyst Vijay Rakesh at Mizuho initiated coverage with a Buy rating and a price target of $50, implying more than 90% upside. The fresh bullish call has reignited interest in quantum computing stocks, which have been battered in recent weeks amid broader market volatility.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Overall, Mizuho's $50 price target values the stock at 11x its projected 2030 price-to-sales ratio. This implies a 20% discount to the firm's DCF valuation, still making it a conservative estimate.

Mizuho's Bullish Call on RGTI

Rakesh pointed to Rigetti's ability to scale as a major reason for its bullish outlook, highlighting the company's roadmap to reach 150+ qubits by 2026 and more than 1,000 qubits by 2027, along with a strong 99.8% two-qubit gate fidelity. Rigetti's 1,000+ qubit system is a next-generation quantum computer designed to tackle complex, real-world problems faster than classical computers.

For context, a qubit is the basic unit of information in a quantum computer. More qubits with low error rates help quantum computers solve problems beyond the reach of today's supercomputers.

He also underscored Rigetti's solid financial footing, with cash and investments enough to potentially fund operations well past 2030. Notably, the company held approximately $600 million in cash, cash equivalents, and available-for-sale investments as of November 6, 2025.

Additionally, Rakesh believes Rigetti is the most comparable smaller company to the big players like IBM (IBM) and Google (GOOGL), when it comes to building scalable quantum computers using superconducting technology.

Is RGTI Stock a Buy?

Rigetti Computing stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among seven Wall Street analysts based on five Buys and two Holds issued in the last three months. The average RGTI price target of $42.17 implies an upside of 57% from current levels.

Year-to-date, RGTI stock is up by over 75%.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: 'It's a Dip, Not a Downtrend,' Says Top Cantor Analyst on Broadcom Stock, Assigns Street-High Price Target
data.description:
Semiconductor company Broadcom (AVGO) slipped after its Q4 FY25 results came with a cautious outlook, but Top Cantor analyst C.J. Muse urged investors to look past the noise. He did not view the drop as a sign of weakness, and reiterated his Overweight rating and Street-high $525 price target, arguing that Broadcom's huge AI backlog and earnings power remain firmly in place despite the market's reaction.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

It is worth noting that Muse ranks 22 out of more than 10,000 analysts tracked by TipRanks. He has a success rate of 72%, with an average return per rating of 49.30% over a one-year timeframe.

Why Cantor Calls Broadcom a Top Pick

Muse said Broadcom beat the October quarter and gave a slight lift to its January guide, with the only drag coming from a higher tax rate. More importantly, he pointed to the $73 billion AI backlog, which includes $53 billion in custom silicon set to be booked over the next six quarters. He said this, along with lead times of six to twelve months, shows steady demand ahead.

He also noted a new $1 billion custom silicon order for 4Q FY26, adding to work with Google (GOOGL), Anthropic, Meta (META), Bytedance, and OAI. That brings Broadcom's core AI customer list to six.

With this pipeline in place, Muse expects AI chip revenue to more than double in 2026, helping EPS reach at least $10.70 next year. He kept his long-term $15 EPS view for CY27. He added that margins are not a major concern right now, since only a small part of Broadcom's AI orders would feel any pressure.

Muse kept Broadcom as a "Top Pick," noting that the recent dip does not change his positive view of the company's AI outlook.

Is AVGO a Good Stock to Buy?

Currently, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Broadcom stock based on 24 Buys and one Hold assigned over the last three months. The average AVGO stock price target of $439.19 indicates 8.08% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Why Is Fermi Stock (FRMI) Down Today?
data.description:
Fermi (FRMI) stock plummeted on Friday after a filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) revealed the end of an agreement with a prospective tenant. The energy infrastructure company announced that the $150 million agreement it had with the first tenant of its Project Matador Site has been terminated. The tenant terminated the agreement after it failed to supply funding within the exclusivity window.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Fermi noted that it is still in talks with this tenant and has also started negotiations with other tenants for the site. These funds are needed for the construction of the energy production facility. Despite these issues, the company said that it is confident it will be able to meet its power delivery schedule at the Project Matador Site. It anticipates construction contracts to meet the growing energy demands of artificial intelligence (AI).

AI has underwent a massive boom in 2025, and this has ramped up production of the data centers that power the technology. With these new data centers comes the need for additional energy. Fermi offers alternative energy sources, such as nuclear, gas, and solar, to power these facilities. This energy is much needed, as some areas have already felt the extra stress that AI data centers have added to their energy grids.

Fermi Stock Movement Today

Fermi stock was down 43.93% in pre-market trading on Friday, following a slight drop yesterday. The stock has also decreased 47.01% year-to-date and has seen the same fall over the past 12 months.

With today's news came heavy trading of FRMI stock. As of this writing, more than 9.9 million shares have changed hands, compared to a three-month daily average of about 3.83 million shares.
data.author: William White

data.title: Tesla Stock Shrugs Off Three-Year EV Sales Low and 30% European Sales Collapse
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) stock is shrugging off bad news, posting a marginal 0.2% gain even as the company faces a wave of negative sales data and deep global headwinds. Despite the difficulties, the stock remains up 11% so far in 2025. The bad news for the car business is piling up: sales in November dropped to a three-year low. Globally, Tesla is likely to see its second consecutive down year, including its first-ever down year in China.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Tesla's Global Sales Slump Hits Three-Year Low

Tesla's sales slump is being driven by problems across all major markets. In the U.S., investors knew the end of the year would be rough, as sales slumped after the removal of the $7,500 federal EV purchase tax credit at the end of September.

Outside the U.S., the problems are worse. Through October, European sales plummeted 30% compared with 2024, totaling just 180,688 cars. This slump is made worse by fierce competition, with China's BYD (1211) seeing a 285% increase in sales in Europe over the same period. Competition in China has also hurt, with sales there down about 6% year over year through November, signaling the deep impact of local rivals.

Why TSLA Stock Ignores The Cars

The resilience of Tesla's stock comes down to the fact that investors have moved on to AI. The stock's valuation is now based on the promise of significant future earnings from its AI businesses, rather than current car sales. Tesla is using AI computing to train cars to drive themselves and to develop humanoid robots.

Moreover, the company has already started a robo-taxi business in June and could be selling some robots as soon as 2026. This massive future potential is enough to keep the stock elevated, regardless of the poor vehicle data.

Is Tesla Stock a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

On TipRanks, TSLA stock has a Hold consensus rating based on 12 Buys, 12 Holds, and 10 Sell ratings. The average Tesla price target of $383.54 implies 14.2% downside potential from current levels.
data.author: Annika Masrani
